artifacts.summary: Blocker investigation: Classical.choose existence proof recommended over iterative construction

next_steps: Run /plan 961 to create implementation plan using Classical.choose approach

findings_summary.approach_1_finite_classes: NOT VIABLE - equivalence classes are not provably finite (GContent-based equivalence can have infinite classes)
findings_summary.approach_2_visited_set: NOT VIABLE - no natural finite candidate set exists (GContent is infinite, subformulaClosure doesn't bound distinguishing formulas)
findings_summary.approach_3_classical_choose: RECOMMENDED - prove existence non-constructively using density property and Classical.choose

key_insight: The iteration termination problem is a proof engineering issue, not a mathematical one. Strict intermediates EXIST by the density property; we need not construct them iteratively.

implementation_strategy.step_1: Prove strict_intermediate_exists_core using contradiction - assume no strict intermediate exists, show quotient collapses
implementation_strategy.step_2: Use intermediate_not_both_equiv to show c cannot be ~ both p and q
implementation_strategy.step_3: Apply Classical.choose to the existence proof
